rules.reason: signed-in users manage the products they created. v1 keeps the catalog owner-scoped like app-invoices; a public read rule for a storefront view is deferred (see dbbasic-package.json)
signed-in users manage the warehouse/zone/aisle/shelf/bin (and virtual customer/supplier) locations they created, same owner-scoped posture as products

signed-in users manage the stock moves they recorded. stock_moves is append-mode and immutable in spirit (see schemas/stock_moves.json) but this platform has no separate create-only permission action -- update/delete stay owner-scoped rather than granted to everyone, the same posture app-thread's append-mode thread_comments takes
the products page is public; it shows a sign-in prompt to visitors
the locations page is public; it shows a sign-in prompt to visitors
the stock levels page is public; it shows a sign-in prompt to visitors, and computes the owner-scoped summary itself once signed in (see objects/site/stock.py)
